literal: transactional, pertaining to vyavahāra (worldly transaction); from vy-ava-√hṛ 'to deal-with, to transact'
invariant_definition: An Advaita technical category — one of the three levels of reality (*sattā-traya*): *pāramārthika* (absolute/ultimate), *vyāvahārika* (transactional/empirical), *prātibhāsika* (apparent-only, like a dream-object or the rope-snake). The *vyāvahārika* level is the level of ordinary waking-state cognition — real for transactional purposes, sublatable in *brahma-jñāna*. The *vyāvahārika* status is essential to the Advaita response to the charge that *māyā*-doctrine reduces to subjective idealism: the world IS real at the *vyāvahārika* level. Non-Advaita schools do not adopt the three-level scheme.

per_school.definition: *Vyāvahārika* reality is intersubjective, lawful, empirically tractable, and *bhāvarūpa* — really there for transactional purposes. It is sublated only by *brahma-jñāna*, not by ordinary perception or by other *vyāvahārika* cognitions. *Īśvara*, *jīva*, the world of Vedic injunction, the *karma*-mechanism — all are *vyāvahārika*.

The three-level scheme is rejected; reality is single-level (*sat*), with degrees of dependence. The Advaita *vyāvahārika* category is read as a verbal evasion of full ontological commitment to the world's reality.

translator_note: 'Empirical' is acceptable but loses the technical contrast with *prātibhāsika*. 'Transactional' is closer. The Advaita three-level scheme is structurally important and should be preserved as a distinct technical apparatus.
